XGColorSubtract

Subtracts two color values to create a new color value.

XGCOLOR* XGColorSubtract(
  XGCOLOR* pOut,
  CONST XGCOLOR* pC1,
  CONST XGCOLOR* pC2
);

Parameters

pOut
[in, out] Pointer to a XGCOLOR structure that is the result of the operation.
pC1
[in] Pointer to a source XGCOLOR structure.
pC2
[in] Pointer to a source XGCOLOR structure.

Return Values

This function returns a pointer to a XGCOLOR structure that is the difference between two color values.

Remarks

The return value for this function is the same value returned in the pOut parameter. In this way, the XGColorSubtract function can be used as a parameter for another function.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in xgmath.h.
  Import Library: Use xgraphics.lib.

See Also

XGColorAdd
